// Urls: Intentionally unset: with Https:Enabled the server drives Kestrel's endpoints from code (HTTPS on HttpsPort), so a 'Urls' here would only be overridden — and logged as a Kestrel 'Overriding address(es)' warning. If you turn Https off, set Urls (or ASPNETCORE_URLS) to choose the HTTP binding.

Https.// Enabled: Serve over TLS. HTTPS-only by default: Kestrel binds HttpsPort (TLS) and, because HttpPort is 0, no cleartext port. A self-signed cert is auto-generated for this host and stored in the machine cert store — distribute its public cert to clients' Trusted Root, or set PfxPath/PfxPassword to use your own (e.g. a domain-CA cert clients already trust). A cert failure degrades to an emergency HTTP listener on 5080 (loopback only; the firewall opens 5443) rather than failing startup.
Https.// RequireHttps: Redirect any plain HTTP to HTTPS and send HSTS. With HttpPort 0 there is no HTTP listener to redirect; this also emits HSTS on HTTPS responses. Set HttpPort > 0 AND RequireHttps false during a migration if you need HTTP and HTTPS to coexist while clients move over.
Https.// SelfSignedValidityYears: Lifetime of the auto-generated self-signed cert (clamped 1..100). 100 years keeps the thumbprint stable for the deployment's life so pinned clients never break on rotation. Ignored when PfxPath supplies your own cert.

Client.// InstallerPath: Absolute or content-root-relative path to the tray installer the /download/client endpoint serves. Blank uses 'client/OpenPrintDeploy.Client.Installer.exe' next to the server exe (where Publish-Server.ps1 puts it).

Updates.// GitHubRepo: owner/repo whose latest GitHub release the admin 'Check for updates' button compares against. Blank disables the check.

Auth.// AdminScheme: How the admin browser UI signs in (production only). 'Basic' = prompt for a domain username/password validated against AD (default). 'Negotiate' = legacy Windows SSO. Set to 'Negotiate' and restart to revert without reinstalling.

Admin.// Groups/Users: Break-glass admin grants honoured IN ADDITION to the Settings page, by domain name. Edit + restart to recover access if the Settings page locks you out. When Groups, Users, GroupSids and the Settings-page list are all empty, any authenticated user is an admin.

Ldap.// Port/UseSsl: LDAPS by default (TLS on 636) so admin credential binds and group lookups aren't sent in cleartext. Requires the DC to present an LDAPS certificate the server trusts (standard with AD Certificate Services). If admin sign-in fails after install, your DC has no trusted LDAPS cert — fix the DC cert, or as a stopgap set UseSsl=false and Port=389 (cleartext) or AllowInvalidCertificate=true.
